stories.headline: ‘Lives turned in a second’: Family of baby Sam, shot dead by Israel, grieve

Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.summary: ‘Fortress North America’ is Ottawa’s response to being cut out of negotiations on new pact with US and Mexico

stories.headline: Mercuria’s First-Half Profit Jumped 88% on Commodity Shocks
stories.summary: Mercuria Energy Group enjoyed an 88% jump in first-half profit, putting it on track for one of its best-ever annual results as the Hormuz crisis creates huge opportunities for commodity traders.

stories.headline: Giant Oil Supertanker Orders Eclipse Record Set in 2008
stories.summary: The world’s shipowners have placed orders for a record number of new oil supertankers, surpassing a boom back in 2008 that ultimately led to a glut and a collapse in rates.

stories.headline: Air France Could Consider Being Part of EasyJet Bid, CEO Says
stories.summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, Air France-KLM CEO Benjamin Smith said the airline may need to raise ticket prices to compensate for rising fuel costs sparked by war in the Middle East. The carrier is also looking to reopen routes to the region when  it's safer. Smith signaled he’d be willing to consider becoming involved in a bid for EasyJet Plc.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Remittances Lift India’s Current Account, Cushion War Shock
stories.summary: India recorded a surprise current account surplus in the January-March period as strong services receipts and higher remittances offset disruptions caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
stories.summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.

stories.summary: Gérald Darmanin will not resign, but he apologised for the handling of the primary suspect in an 11-year-old girl's murder and asked France's public prosecutors to re-examine the country's 70,000 legal complaints under review for alleged crimes against children.

stories.headline: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks

stories.headline: Amazon to Raise C$14 Billion in Record Canadian Dollar Bond Sale
stories.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to raise C$14 billion ($10 billion) from investment-grade bonds in Canadian dollars, the largest such offering on record, as US hyperscalers look beyond their home market to fund artificial intelligence spending.

stories.headline: Strategy Resumes Buying Bitcoin After Rare Sale Roils Crypto
stories.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. resumed buying Bitcoin a week after a rare token sale by the digital asset treasury company roiled the crypto market.

stories.headline: Blue Owl Fund Hit by Redemptions Set to Sell $500 Million Bond
stories.summary: One of Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s private credit funds that capped redemptions earlier this year is set to raise $500 million from an investment-grade bond sale.

stories.headline: Drahi Reaches €20 Billion Deal to Sell Phone Carrier SFR
stories.summary: Billionaire Patrick Drahi has reached an agreement to sell French mobile carrier SFR to a consortium of rival telecommunications firms, in a deal that values the company €20.4 billion ($23.5 billion) and will test regulators’ tolerance for consolidation.

stories.headline: Private Share Marketplace Hiive Shops Equity as IPO Boom Nears
stories.summary: The Hiive Co., an online platform for trading shares in pre-public companies, recently held talks with investors about a secondary stock sale that would have valued the firm at about $780 million, according to a document seen by Bloomberg.

stories.summary: The easiest way to understand crypto used to be to look at Bitcoin.

stories.headline: US Steel Sees Pennsylvania Overhaul Costing Up to $2.5 Billion
stories.summary: US Steel said investments to upgrade its Mon Valley operations in Pennsylvania could total as much as $2.5 billion, more than double Nippon Steel Corp.’s minimum commitment as part of its takeover offer for the American company.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Monsoon season brings vital rainfall to parts of Asia

stories.headline: Hegseth attacks Europe over migration with beach 'invasion' D-Day speech
stories.summary: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

stories.summary: When pressed by host Kristen Welker, the president cited no evidence for claims about Jan. 6 and elections he said were “rigged.”

stories.summary: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

stories.summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: Indonesia's Prabowo fires head of free meals scheme plagued by poisonings
stories.summary: The centrepiece programme of Prabowo's government has left tens of thousands of school children ill.

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain

stories.headline: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico
